emptyGroupsWhy: Empty groups clutter AD and may still be referenced in UNC path ACLs or GPOs, causing confusion during audits.
emptyGroupsFix: Delete the group if unused, or add the appropriate members. Check UNC permissions before deleting.
emptyGroupsNote: The group may still be configured on UNC paths or file share ACLs. Verify with NTFS Analyzer before deleting.
singleMemberWhy: A single-member group adds unnecessary indirection. The user could be assigned permissions directly.
singleMemberFix: Assign the user directly to the resource, then remove the group. Or add more members if the group is meant to grow.
singleMemberNote: Some applications require group-based access even for a single user. Verify before removing.
staleGroupsDesc: Groups not modified in over 180 days.
staleGroupsWhy: Stale groups may reflect outdated team structures or completed projects, increasing the attack surface.
staleGroupsFix: Review with the group owner. Archive or delete if obsolete, update membership if still needed.
noDescriptionWhy: Without a description, administrators cannot determine the group's purpose, making audits and cleanup harder.
noDescriptionFix: Add a meaningful description in Group Management (e.g. team name, project, access scope).
circularNestingDesc: Groups that contain each other in a nesting loop (A contains B contains A).
circularNestingWhy: Circular nesting causes unexpected permission inheritance and can degrade LDAP query performance.
circularNestingFix: Break the cycle by removing one of the nesting relationships. Decide which group should be the parent.
circularNestingNote: Circular nesting can cause token bloat and authentication delays. Fix as soon as possible.
excessiveDepthWhy: Deep nesting makes permissions hard to audit and can cause Kerberos token size issues (MaxTokenSize).
excessiveDepthFix: Flatten the group structure by reducing nesting levels. Consider using direct memberships instead.
duplicateGroupsDesc: Multiple groups with exactly the same set of members.
duplicateGroupsWhy: Duplicate groups create confusion about which group to use and increase maintenance burden.
duplicateGroupsFix: Consolidate into a single group. Update all ACLs and GPOs to reference the surviving group, then delete duplicates.
duplicateGroupsNote: Different groups may be used on different resources. Verify all references before merging.
deleteDialogWarning: This action cannot be undone. The following groups will be permanently deleted:
scannerDesc1: Empty groups - no members at all
scannerDesc2: Single-member groups - only one member, possibly redundant
scannerDesc3: Stale groups - not modified in over 180 days
scannerDesc5: Circular nesting - Group A contains B contains A
scannerDesc6: Excessive depth - nested deeper than 3 levels
scannerBulkDelete: Empty groups can be deleted in bulk (DomainAdmin required).
